title: Network Security, Attacks, Segmentation, and Hardening · PBQ Checkpoint
summary: Threat-model and harden enterprise, guest, IoT, OT, management, and remote-access network zones. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a defense-in-depth network security plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish confidentiality, integrity, availability, risk, threat, vulnerability, exploit, likelihood, and impact.
Protect data in transit and at rest and explain certificates, PKI trust, self-signed limits, key management, and rotation.
Use MFA, SSO, RADIUS, LDAP, SAML, TACACS+, time-based authentication, authorization, RBAC, and least privilege appropriately.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

stimulus.title: Net Acl Filtering failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: An ACL automatically evaluates the most specific rule before earlier rules.

lines.label: required

options.text: Accept the result because visible output is the highest authority.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
Run the boundary case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Net Cia Risk Terms evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Net Cia Risk Terms assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Distinguish confidentiality, integrity, availability, risk, threat, vulnerability, exploit, likelihood, and impact. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: network security. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Net Cia Risk Terms, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a complete first version of a defense-in-depth network security plan while every earlier competency remains active.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A vulnerability is the same thing as an active attacker exploiting it. This fails the Net Cia Risk Terms contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Net Acl Filtering applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Design ordered ACL, URL, and content filtering rules with least privilege, state, direction, logging, and exception review.
